codeReview.summary: Audit confirmed no analytics SDKs, no telemetry endpoints, and only HuggingFace model downloads as external network calls. All AI inference (LLM via llama-cpp-python, STT via faster-whisper, TTS via piper-tts) runs locally. The only external network call is model_management.py downloading GGUF models from HuggingFace via huggingface_hub, which is explicitly permitted.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md file explicitly states 'Do not add any analytics or telemetry' in Off-Limits section, but does not list this as an explicit convention. The worker followed this guidance correctly. No gap in documentation needed - the convention is clear.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md lists 'Do not add any analytics or telemetry' under Off-Limits

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly identified that the old servers/ directory contains edge_tts which makes external Microsoft calls, but this is the old TypeScript/React reference codebase and excluded per mission guidance. The audit correctly scoped only the new backend-python/ code.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md states 'Do not modify existing TypeScript/React codebase (reference only)'

summary: Feature privacy-no-analytics PASSED review. Code audit confirms zero analytics, telemetry, or tracking code in the new Flet/Python codebase. Only external network calls are HuggingFace model downloads via huggingface_hub. All AI inference runs locally. No issues found.
